1→/-
     2→Copyright 2025 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3→

    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.

    16→
    17→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Acyclic
    18→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    19→import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    20→import Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean
    21→import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
    22→
    23→namespace SimpleGraph
    24→
    25→open Classical Finset List
    26→
    27→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    28→
    29→/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30→It is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31→noncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    32→  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ∧ IsTree T.coe }
    33→  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34→  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35→  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ℝ)) spanningTrees)
    36→
    37→/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38→noncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := Fintype.card α
    39→
    40→/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41→noncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    42→  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43→  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ℝ)) matchings)
    44→
    45→/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46→    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47→    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48→noncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    49→  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset α) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set α))
    50→  letI diff (s : Finset α) : ℤ := (s.card : ℤ) - (⋃ v ∈ (s : Set α), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51→  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52→  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ↦ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53→  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54→  (max_card.getD 0 : ℝ)
    55→
    56→/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57→noncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    58→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ∧ s.card = n }
    59→
    60→/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61→noncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ℕ :=
    62→  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : α => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
    63→
    64→/--
    65→The maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66→-/
    67→noncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
    68→  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69→  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70→
    71→/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72→bipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73→noncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    74→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ∧ s.card = n }
    75→
    76→/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77→Returned as a real number. -/
    78→noncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    79→  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ℝ)
    80→
    81→/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82→noncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ :=
    83→  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84→
    85→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86→noncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℝ :=
    87→  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ℝ)
    88→
    89→/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90→noncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    91→  (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    92→
    93→/-- A unit distance graph in ℝ²:
    94→A graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ℝ² and there is
    95→an edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96→def UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97→  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98→  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99→
   100→/--
   101→Two walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102→-/
   103→def InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104→    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105→  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106→
   107→/--
   108→We say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109→pairwise disjoint paths.
   110→-/
   111→def InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112→  Pairwise fun u v ↦ ∃ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113→    P.Infinite ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, p.IsPath) ∧ P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114→
   115→/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116→degree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117→`∞` rather than 0.
   118→-/
   119→noncomputable
   120→def edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ℕ∞ := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121→
   122→noncomputable
   123→def emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ v, G.edegree v
   124→
   125→noncomputable
   126→def ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127→
   128→end SimpleGraph
   129→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 81
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/81](https://www.erdosproblems.com/81)
    23→
    24→Can the edges of a chordal graph on $n$ vertices be partitioned into at most
    25→$n^2/6 + O(n)$ cliques?
    26→
    27→[EOZ93] Erdős, P., Ordman, E.T., and Zalcstein, Y., *Clique partitions of chordal graphs*,
    28→Combinatorics, Probability and Computing **2** (1993), 409-415.
    29→-/
    30→
    31→open SimpleGraph
    32→
    33→namespace Erdos81
    34→
    35→/--
    36→An induced cycle of length $k$ in a simple graph $G$: an injective map
    37→$f : \text{Fin}\ k \to V$ such that $f(i)$ and $f(j)$ are adjacent if and only if
    38→$i$ and $j$ are consecutive modulo $k$. This captures the notion that $f$ traces out a
    39→cycle with no chords.
    40→-/
    41→def HasInducedCycle {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ℕ) : Prop :=
    42→  ∃ f : Fin k → V, Function.Injective f ∧
    43→    ∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j →
    44→      (G.Adj (f i) (f j) ↔ (i.val + 1) % k = j.val ∨ (j.val + 1) % k = i.val)
    45→
    46→/--
    47→A graph is chordal if it contains no induced cycle of length $\geq 4$.
    48→Equivalently, every cycle of length $\geq 4$ has a chord.
    49→-/
    50→def IsChordal {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
    51→  ∀ k : ℕ, 4 ≤ k → ¬HasInducedCycle G k
    52→
    53→/--
    54→Erdős Problem 81 (Erdős, Ordman, Zalcstein [EOZ93]):
    55→
    56→Let $G$ be a chordal graph on $n$ vertices — that is, $G$ has no induced cycles of
    57→length greater than 3. Can the edges of $G$ be partitioned into $n^2/6 + O(n)$
    58→many cliques?
    59→
    60→The example of all edges between a complete graph on $n/3$ vertices and an empty
    61→graph on $2n/3$ vertices shows that $n^2/6 + O(n)$ is sometimes necessary.
    62→
    63→Formalized as: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $n$ and all
    64→chordal graphs $G$ on $n$ vertices, there exists a collection $P$ of cliques
    65→(vertex sets, each pairwise adjacent in $G$) that partition the edges of $G$,
    66→with $|P| \leq n^2/6 + Cn$.
    67→-/
    68→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    69→theorem erdos_81 : answer(sorry) ↔
    70→    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
    71→    ∀ n : ℕ, ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    72→      IsChordal G →
    73→      ∃ P : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    74→        -- Each set in P is a clique in G
    75→        (∀ S ∈ P, G.IsClique (↑S : Set (Fin n))) ∧
    76→        -- Every edge of G is covered by some clique in P
    77→        (∀ u v : Fin n, G.Adj u v → ∃ S ∈ P, u ∈ S ∧ v ∈ S) ∧
    78→        -- No edge belongs to two distinct cliques (partition, not just cover)
    79→        (∀ S₁ ∈ P, ∀ S₂ ∈ P, S₁ ≠ S₂ →
    80→          ∀ u v : Fin n, u ∈ S₁ → v ∈ S₁ → u ∈ S₂ → v ∈ S₂ → ¬G.Adj u v) ∧
    81→        -- The number of cliques is at most n²/6 + C·n
    82→        (P.card : ℝ) ≤ (n : ℝ) ^ 2 / 6 + C * (n : ℝ) := by
    83→  sorry
    84→
    85→end Erdos81
    86→

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 807
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/807](https://www.erdosproblems.com/807)
    23→
    24→Is it true that for a random graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with edge probability $1/2$,
    25→$\tau(G) = n - \alpha(G)$ almost surely? Here $\tau(G)$ is the bipartition number (the
    26→smallest number of pairwise edge-disjoint complete bipartite graphs whose union is $G$)
    27→and $\alpha(G)$ is the independence number. Disproved by Alon [Al15].
    28→
    29→[Al15] Alon, N., _Bipartite decomposition of random graphs_, J. Graph Theory, 2015.
    30→
    31→[ABH17] Alon, N., Bohman, T. and Huang, H., _More on bipartite decomposition of random
    32→graphs_, J. Graph Theory, 2017.
    33→-/
    34→
    35→open SimpleGraph Filter Classical
    36→
    37→open scoped Topology
    38→
    39→namespace Erdos807
    40→
    41→/--
    42→A biclique cover of a simple graph $G$ of size $k$ consists of $k$ complete bipartite
    43→subgraphs (specified by pairs of disjoint vertex sets) that are pairwise
    44→edge-disjoint and together cover all edges of $G$.
    45→-/
    46→def IsBicliqueCover {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ℕ)
    47→    (A B : Fin k → Set V) : Prop :=
    48→  (∀ i, Disjoint (A i) (B i)) ∧
    49→  (∀ i, ∀ a ∈ A i, ∀ b ∈ B i, G.Adj a b) ∧
    50→  (∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j →
    51→    ∀ v w : V, ¬((v ∈ A i ∧ w ∈ B i ∨ w ∈ A i ∧ v ∈ B i) ∧
    52→                  (v ∈ A j ∧ w ∈ B j ∨ w ∈ A j ∧ v ∈ B j))) ∧
    53→  (∀ v w : V, G.Adj v w →
    54→    ∃ i : Fin k, (v ∈ A i ∧ w ∈ B i) ∨ (w ∈ A i ∧ v ∈ B i))
    55→
    56→/--
    57→The bipartition number $\tau(G)$ is the minimum number of pairwise edge-disjoint
    58→complete bipartite subgraphs needed to cover all edges of $G$.
    59→-/
    60→noncomputable def bipartitionNumber {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ :=
    61→  sInf {k : ℕ | ∃ (A B : Fin k → Set V), IsBicliqueCover G k A B}
    62→
    63→/--
    64→The independence number $\alpha(G)$ of a finite graph is the maximum cardinality of
    65→an independent set (a set of vertices with no edges between them).
    66→-/
    67→noncomputable def independenceNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V] (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ :=
    68→  sSup {k : ℕ | ∃ S : Finset V, S.card = k ∧
    69→    ∀ v ∈ S, ∀ w ∈ S, v ≠ w → ¬G.Adj v w}
    70→
    71→/--
    72→Erdős Problem 807 (disproved by Alon [Al15]):
    73→
    74→Is it true that for a random graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with edge probability $1/2$,
    75→$\tau(G) = n - \alpha(G)$ almost surely?
    76→
    77→The bipartition number $\tau(G)$ is the smallest number of pairwise edge-disjoint
    78→complete bipartite graphs whose union is $G$. The independence number $\alpha(G)$ is
    79→the size of the largest independent set in $G$.
    80→
    81→In the $G(n, 1/2)$ model every labeled graph on $n$ vertices is equally likely, so
    82→"almost surely" means the proportion of graphs satisfying the property tends
    83→to $1$ as $n \to \infty$.
    84→
    85→Alon [Al15] showed this is false: almost surely $\tau(G) \leq n - \alpha(G) - 1$.
    86→Alon, Bohman, and Huang [ABH17] proved there exists $c > 0$ such that
    87→almost surely $\tau(G) \leq n - (1 + c)\alpha(G)$.
    88→-/
    89→@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]
    90→theorem erdos_807 : answer(False) ↔
    91→    Tendsto (fun n : ℕ =>
    92→      (Nat.card {G : SimpleGraph (Fin n) //
    93→        bipartitionNumber G = n - independenceNumber G} : ℝ) /
    94→      (Nat.card (SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℝ))
    95→    atTop (nhds 1) := by
    96→  sorry
    97→
    98→end Erdos807
    99→

    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 1017
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1017](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1017)
    23→
    24→Let $f(n,k)$ be such that every graph on $n$ vertices and $k$ edges can be
    25→partitioned into at most $f(n,k)$ edge-disjoint complete graphs. Estimate
    26→$f(n,k)$ for $k > n^2/4$.
    27→
    28→The function $f(n,k)$ is sometimes called the clique partition number.
    29→
    30→Erdős, Goodman, and Pósa [EGP66] proved that $f(n,k) \le \lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$ for all $k$
    31→(and in fact edges and triangles suffice), which is best possible in general,
    32→as witnessed by a complete bipartite graph. Erdős [Er71] asks whether this
    33→bound can be sharpened when $k > n^2/4$.
    34→
    35→[EGP66] Erdős, P., Goodman, A. W., and Pósa, L., _The representation of a graph by set
    36→intersections_. Canadian Journal of Mathematics 18 (1966), 106-112.
    37→
    38→[Er71] Erdős, P., _Some unsolved problems in graph theory and combinatorial analysis_.
    39→Combinatorial Mathematics and its Applications (Proc. Conf., Oxford, 1969) (1971), 97-109.
    40→-/
    41→
    42→open SimpleGraph Finset Filter
    43→
    44→namespace Erdos1017
    45→
    46→/--
    47→Erdős Problem 1017 [Er71]:
    48→
    49→Erdős asks whether the clique partition number $f(n,k)$ can be improved
    50→below $\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$ when the number of edges $k$ exceeds $n^2/4$.
    51→-/
    52→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    53→theorem erdos_1017 : answer(sorry) ↔
    54→    ∀ᶠ n in atTop,
    55→      ∀ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj),
    56→        haveI := dG;
    57→        G.edgeFinset.card > n ^ 2 / 4 →
    58→          ∃ (k : ℕ) (parts : Fin k → Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))),
    59→            k < n ^ 2 / 4 ∧
    60→            (∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j → Disjoint (parts i) (parts j)) ∧
    61→            (∀ e, e ∈ G.edgeFinset ↔ ∃ i, e ∈ parts i) ∧
    62→            (∀ i : Fin k, ∃ (S : Finset (Fin n)),
    63→              G.IsClique (↑S : Set (Fin n)) ∧
    64→              parts i = S.offDiag.image (Quot.mk _)) := by
    65→  sorry
    66→
    67→/--
    68→Erdős–Goodman–Pósa theorem [EGP66]:
    69→
    70→Every simple graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into at most
    71→$\lfloor n^2/4 \rfloor$ edge-disjoint complete subgraphs.
    72→-/
    73→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    74→theorem erdos_1017.variants.erdos_goodman_posa :
    75→    ∀ (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj),
    76→      haveI := dG;
    77→      ∃ (k : ℕ) (parts : Fin k → Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))),
    78→        k ≤ n ^ 2 / 4 ∧
    79→        (∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j → Disjoint (parts i) (parts j)) ∧
    80→        (∀ e, e ∈ G.edgeFinset ↔ ∃ i, e ∈ parts i) ∧
    81→        (∀ i : Fin k, ∃ (S : Finset (Fin n)),
    82→          G.IsClique (↑S : Set (Fin n)) ∧
    83→          parts i = S.offDiag.image (Quot.mk _)) := by

    18→# Graph cliques
    19→
    20→This file defines cliques in simple graphs.
    21→A clique is a set of vertices that are pairwise adjacent.
    22→
    23→## Main declarations
    24→
    25→* `SimpleGraph.IsClique`: Predicate for a set of vertices to be a clique.
    26→* `SimpleGraph.IsNClique`: Predicate for a set of vertices to be an `n`-clique.
    27→* `SimpleGraph.cliqueFinset`: Finset of `n`-cliques of a graph.
    28→* `SimpleGraph.CliqueFree`: Predicate for a graph to have no `n`-cliques.

    31→@[expose] public section

    35→namespace SimpleGraph
    36→

    39→/-! ### Cliques -/
    40→
    41→
    42→section Clique
    43→
    44→variable {s t : Set α}
    45→
    46→/-- A clique in a graph is a set of vertices that are pairwise adjacent. -/
    47→abbrev IsClique (s : Set α) : Prop :=
    48→  s.Pairwise G.Adj
    49→
    50→theorem isClique_iff : G.IsClique s ↔ s.Pairwise G.Adj :=
    51→  Iff.rfl
    52→
    53→lemma not_isClique_iff : ¬ G.IsClique s ↔ ∃ (v w : s), v ≠ w ∧ ¬ G.Adj v w := by
    54→  aesop (add simp [isClique_iff, Set.Pairwise])
    55→
    56→/-- A clique is a set of vertices whose induced graph is complete. -/
    57→theorem isClique_iff_induce_eq : G.IsClique s ↔ G.induce s = ⊤ := by
    58→  rw [isClique_iff]
    59→  constructor
    60→  · intro h
    61→    ext ⟨v, hv⟩ ⟨w, hw⟩
    62→    simp only [comap_adj, top_adj, Ne, Subtype.mk_eq_mk]
    63→    exact ⟨Adj.ne, h hv hw⟩
    64→  · intro h v hv w hw hne
    65→    have h2 : (G.induce s).Adj ⟨v, hv⟩ ⟨w, hw⟩ = _ := rfl
    66→    conv_lhs at h2 => rw [h]
    67→    simp only [top_adj, ne_eq, Subtype.mk.injEq, eq_iff_iff] at h2
    68→    exact h2.1 hne
    69→
    70→theorem isClique_iff_isChain_adj : G.IsClique s ↔ IsChain G.Adj s := by
    71→  simp [IsChain, G.symm.iff]
    72→
    73→instance [DecidableEq α] [DecidableRel G.Adj] {s : Finset α} : Decidable (G.IsClique s) :=
    74→  decidable_of_iff' _ G.isClique_iff

    79→

    81→

    83→  hs.pairwise G.Adj
    84→
    85→lemma isClique_pair : G.IsClique {a, b} ↔ a ≠ b → G.Adj a b := Set.pairwise_pair_of_symmetric G.symm
    86→
    87→@[simp]
    88→lemma isClique_insert : G.IsClique (insert a s) ↔ G.IsClique s ∧ ∀ b ∈ s, a ≠ b → G.Adj a b :=
    89→  Set.pairwise_insert_of_symmetric G.symm
    90→
    91→lemma isClique_insert_of_notMem (ha : a ∉ s) :
    92→    G.IsClique (insert a s) ↔ G.IsClique s ∧ ∀ b ∈ s, G.Adj a b :=
    93→  Set.pairwise_insert_of_symmetric_of_notMem G.symm ha

    96→    G.IsClique (insert a s) := hs.insert_of_symmetric G.symm h

   213→
   214→theorem isNClique_iff : G.IsNClique n s ↔ G.IsClique s ∧ #s = n :=
   215→  ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨h.1, h.2⟩, fun h ↦ ⟨h.1, h.2⟩⟩
   216→
   217→instance [DecidableEq α] [DecidableRel G.Adj] {n : ℕ} {s : Finset α} :
   218→    Decidable (G.IsNClique n s) :=
   219→  decidable_of_iff' _ G.isNClique_iff
   220→
   221→variable {G H} {a b c : α}
   222→
   223→@[simp] lemma isNClique_empty : G.IsNClique n ∅ ↔ n = 0 := by simp [isNClique_iff, eq_comm]
   224→
   225→@[simp]
   226→lemma isNClique_singleton : G.IsNClique n {a} ↔ n = 1 := by simp [isNClique_iff, eq_comm]
   227→
   228→theorem IsNClique.mono (h : G ≤ H) : G.IsNClique n s → H.IsNClique n s := by
   229→  simp_rw [isNClique_iff]
   230→  exact And.imp_left (IsClique.mono h)
   231→
   232→protected theorem IsNClique.map (h : G.IsNClique n s) {f : α ↪ β} :
   233→    (G.map f).IsNClique n (s.map f) :=
   234→  ⟨by rw [coe_map]; exact h.1.map, (card_map _).trans h.2⟩
   235→
   236→theorem isNClique_map_iff (hn : 1 < n) {t : Finset β} {f : α ↪ β} :
   237→    (G.map f).IsNClique n t ↔ ∃ s : Finset α, G.IsNClique n s ∧ s.map f = t := by
   238→  rw [isNClique_iff, isClique_map_finset_iff, or_and_right,
   239→    or_iff_right (by rintro ⟨h', rfl⟩; exact h'.not_gt hn)]
   240→  constructor
   241→  · rintro ⟨⟨s, hs, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
   242→    simp [isNClique_iff, hs]
   243→  rintro ⟨s, hs, rfl⟩
   244→  simp [hs.card_eq, hs.isClique]
   245→
   246→@[simp]
   247→theorem isNClique_bot_iff : (⊥ : SimpleGraph α).IsNClique n s ↔ n ≤ 1 ∧ #s = n := by
   248→  rw [isNClique_iff, isClique_bot_iff]
   249→  refine and_congr_left ?_
   250→  rintro rfl
   251→  exact card_le_one.symm
   252→
   253→@[simp]
   254→theorem isNClique_zero : G.IsNClique 0 s ↔ s = ∅ := by
   255→  simp only [isNClique_iff, Finset.card_eq_zero, and_iff_right_iff_imp]; rintro rfl; simp
   256→
   257→@[simp]
   258→theorem isNClique_one : G.IsNClique 1 s ↔ ∃ a, s = {a} := by
   259→  simp only [isNClique_iff, card_eq_one, and_iff_right_iff_imp]; rintro ⟨a, rfl⟩; simp
   260→
   261→section DecidableEq
   262→
   263→variable [DecidableEq α]
   264→
   265→protected theorem IsNClique.insert (hs : G.IsNClique n s) (h : ∀ b ∈ s, G.Adj a b) :
   266→    G.IsNClique (n + 1) (insert a s) := by
   267→  constructor
   268→  · push_cast
   269→    exact hs.1.insert fun b hb _ => h _ hb
   270→  · rw [card_insert_of_notMem fun ha => (h _ ha).ne rfl, hs.2]
   271→
   272→lemma IsNClique.erase_of_mem (hs : G.IsNClique n s) (ha : a ∈ s) :
   273→    G.IsNClique (n - 1) (s.erase a) where
   274→  isClique := hs.isClique.subset <| by simp
   275→  card_eq := by rw [card_erase_of_mem ha, hs.2]
   276→
   277→protected lemma IsNClique.insert_erase
   278→    (hs : G.IsNClique n s) (ha : ∀ w ∈ s \ {b}, G.Adj a w) (hb : b ∈ s) :
   279→    G.IsNClique n (insert a (erase s b)) := by
   280→  cases n with
   281→  | zero => exact False.elim <| notMem_empty _ (isNClique_zero.1 hs ▸ hb)
   282→  | succ _ => exact (hs.erase_of_mem hb).insert fun w h ↦ by aesop
   283→
   284→theorem is3Clique_triple_iff : G.IsNClique 3 {a, b, c} ↔ G.Adj a b ∧ G.Adj a c ∧ G.Adj b c := by
   285→  simp only [isNClique_iff, Set.pairwise_insert_of_symmetric G.symm, coe_insert]
   286→  by_cases hab : a = b <;> by_cases hbc : b = c <;> by_cases hac : a = c <;> subst_vars <;>
   287→    simp [and_rotate, *]
   288→
   289→theorem is3Clique_iff :
   290→    G.IsNClique 3 s ↔ ∃ a b c, G.Adj a b ∧ G.Adj a c ∧ G.Adj b c ∧ s = {a, b, c} := by
   291→  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, ?_⟩
   292→  · obtain ⟨a, b, c, -, -, -, hs⟩ := card_eq_three.1 h.card_eq
   293→    refine ⟨a, b, c, ?_⟩
   294→    rwa [hs, eq_self_iff_true, and_true, is3Clique_triple_iff.symm, ← hs]
   295→  · rintro ⟨a, b, c, hab, hbc, hca, rfl⟩
   296→    exact is3Clique_triple_iff.2 ⟨hab, hbc, hca⟩
   297→
   298→end DecidableEq
   299→

    24→/--
    25→This proposition asserts the ordinal Ramsey property `α → (β, c)²`.
    26→
    27→It states that for any 2-coloring of the complete graph on the ordinal `α`,
    28→one of the following must hold:
    29→* There is a red clique which is order-isomorphic to `β`.
    30→* There is a blue clique of cardinality `c`.

    32→def OrdinalCardinalRamsey (α β : Ordinal.{u}) (c : Cardinal.{u}) : Prop :=
    33→  -- For any 2-coloring of `α`,
    34→  ∀ red blue : SimpleGraph α.ToType, IsCompl red blue →
    35→    -- either there is a red `K_β`
    36→    (∃ s, red.IsClique s ∧ typeLT s = β) ∨
    37→   -- or there is a blue `K_c`.
    38→    ∃ s, blue.IsClique s ∧ #s = c

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 813
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/813](https://www.erdosproblems.com/813)
    23→
    24→A problem of Erdős and Hajnal [Er91], who proved $n^{1/3} \ll h(n) \ll n^{1/2}$.
    25→Bucić and Sudakov [BuSu23] proved $h(n) \gg n^{5/12 - o(1)}$.
    26→
    27→[Er91] Erdős, P., _Problems and results on graphs and hypergraphs: similarities and
    28→differences_. Mathematics of Ramsey theory, Algorithms Combin., 5 (1990), 12-28.
    29→
    30→[BuSu23] Bucić, M. and Sudakov, B., _Tight Ramsey-type results for cliques vs large
    31→almost monochromatic subsets_. (2023).
    32→-/
    33→
    34→open SimpleGraph Classical
    35→
    36→namespace Erdos813
    37→
    38→/-- A graph on $n$ vertices has the property that every subset of $7$ vertices
    39→    contains a triangle (three mutually adjacent vertices). -/
    40→def EverySevenSetHasTriangle {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    41→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = 7 →
    42→    ∃ a b c, a ∈ S ∧ b ∈ S ∧ c ∈ S ∧
    43→      a ≠ b ∧ a ≠ c ∧ b ≠ c ∧
    44→      G.Adj a b ∧ G.Adj a c ∧ G.Adj b c
    45→
    46→/-- The clique number of a graph on $n$ vertices: the supremum of sizes of cliques. -/
    47→noncomputable def graphCliqueNumber {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
    48→  sSup {k : ℕ | ∃ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = k ∧ G.IsClique (↑S : Set (Fin n))}
    49→
    50→/-- $h(n)$ is the minimum clique number over all graphs on $n$ vertices in which
    51→    every set of $7$ vertices contains a triangle. -/
    52→noncomputable def erdos813H (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    53→  sInf (graphCliqueNumber '' {G : SimpleGraph (Fin n) | EverySevenSetHasTriangle G})
    54→
    55→/--
    56→Erdős Problem 813 [Er91]:
    57→
    58→There exist constants $c_1, c_2 > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    59→$$
    60→  C_1 \cdot n^{1/3 + c_1} \leq h(n) \leq C_2 \cdot n^{1/2 - c_2}.
    61→$$
    62→-/
    63→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    64→theorem erdos_813 :
    65→    ∃ c₁ : ℝ, c₁ > 0 ∧
    66→    ∃ c₂ : ℝ, c₂ > 0 ∧
    67→    ∃ C₁ : ℝ, C₁ > 0 ∧
    68→    ∃ C₂ : ℝ, C₂ > 0 ∧
    69→    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ N₀ →
    70→      C₁ * (n : ℝ) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / 3 + c₁) ≤ (erdos813H n : ℝ) ∧
    71→      (erdos813H n : ℝ) ≤ C₂ * (n : ℝ) ^ ((1 : ℝ) / 2 - c₂) := by
    72→  sorry
    73→
    74→end Erdos813

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 610
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/610](https://www.erdosproblems.com/610)
    23→
    24→For a graph $G$, let $\tau(G)$ denote the minimal number of vertices that include at
    25→least one from each maximal clique of $G$ (the clique transversal number).
    26→
    27→Estimate $\tau(G)$. In particular, is it true that if $G$ has $n$ vertices then
    28→$\tau(G) \leq n - \omega(n)\sqrt{n}$ for some $\omega(n) \to \infty$, or even
    29→$\tau(G) \leq n - c\sqrt{n \log n}$ for some absolute constant $c > 0$?
    30→
    31→A problem of Erdős, Gallai, and Tuza [EGT92], who proved that
    32→$\tau(G) \leq n - \sqrt{2n} + O(1)$.
    33→
    34→[EGT92] Erdős, P., Gallai, T., and Tuza, Zs., *Covering the cliques of a graph with
    35→vertices*, Discrete Mathematics, 1992.
    36→
    37→[Er94] Erdős, P., *Some old and new problems in various branches of combinatorics*,
    38→Discrete Mathematics, 1994.
    39→
    40→[Er99] Erdős, P., *Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics*,
    41→Le Matematiche, 1999.
    42→-/
    43→
    44→open SimpleGraph Filter
    45→
    46→namespace Erdos610
    47→
    48→/-- $S$ is a maximal clique of $G$ (as a `Finset`): it is a clique and no vertex
    49→outside $S$ can be added while preserving the clique property. -/
    50→def IsMaximalCliqueFS {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    51→  G.IsClique (S : Set (Fin n)) ∧
    52→  ∀ v : Fin n, v ∉ S → ¬G.IsClique (↑(insert v S) : Set (Fin n))
    53→
    54→/-- $T$ is a clique transversal of $G$ if $T$ meets every maximal clique of $G$
    55→that has at least $2$ vertices. -/
    56→def IsCliqueTransversal {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (T : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    57→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), IsMaximalCliqueFS G S → 2 ≤ S.card → (T ∩ S).Nonempty
    58→
    59→/-- The clique transversal number $\tau(G)$: the minimum cardinality of a clique
    60→transversal of $G$. -/
    61→noncomputable def cliqueTransversalNum {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
    62→  sInf { k : ℕ | ∃ T : Finset (Fin n), IsCliqueTransversal G T ∧ T.card = k }
    63→
    64→/--
    65→**Erdős Problem 610** (Weak form) [EGT92]:
    66→
    67→There exists a function $\omega : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{R}$ with $\omega(n) \to \infty$
    68→such that for every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices,
    69→$\tau(G) \leq n - \omega(n) \cdot \sqrt{n}$.
    70→-/
    71→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    72→theorem erdos_610 : answer(sorry) ↔
    73→    ∃ ω : ℕ → ℝ, Tendsto ω atTop atTop ∧
    74→      ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ 1 →
    75→        ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    76→          (cliqueTransversalNum G : ℝ) ≤ (n : ℝ) - ω n * Real.sqrt (n : ℝ) := by
    77→  sorry
    78→
    79→/--
    80→**Erdős Problem 610** (Strong form) [EGT92] [Er94] [Er99]:
    81→
    82→There exists $c > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices,
    83→$\tau(G) \leq n - c \cdot \sqrt{n \cdot \log n}$.
    84→-/
    85→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    86→theorem erdos_610.variants.strong : answer(sorry) ↔
    87→    ∃ c : ℝ, c > 0 ∧
    88→      ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ 2 →
    89→        ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    90→          (cliqueTransversalNum G : ℝ) ≤
    91→            (n : ℝ) - c * Real.sqrt ((n : ℝ) * Real.log (n : ℝ)) := by
    92→  sorry
    93→
    94→/--
    95→**Erdős Problem 610** (Known bound, Erdős–Gallai–Tuza) [EGT92]:
    96→
    97→There exists $C > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices,
    98→$\tau(G) \leq n - \sqrt{2n} + C$.
    99→-/
   100→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   101→theorem erdos_610.variants.known_bound :
   102→    ∃ C : ℝ, ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ 1 →
   103→      ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
   104→        (cliqueTransversalNum G : ℝ) ≤ (n : ℝ) - Real.sqrt (2 * (n : ℝ)) + C := by
   105→  sorry
   106→
   107→end Erdos610
   108→

    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 611
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/611](https://www.erdosproblems.com/611)
    23→
    24→Let $\tau(G)$ denote the minimum number of vertices needed to intersect every maximal
    25→clique of a graph $G$. Erdős, Gallai, and Tuza conjectured that if every maximal clique
    26→of $G$ on $n$ vertices has at least $cn$ vertices (for a fixed constant $c > 0$), then
    27→$\tau(G) = o(n)$.
    28→
    29→[EGT92] Erdős, P., Gallai, T., and Tuza, Z., _Covering the cliques of a graph with vertices_.
    30→Discrete Mathematics (1992).
    31→
    32→[Er94] Erdős, P., _Some old and new problems in various branches of combinatorics_.
    33→Discrete Mathematics (1994).
    34→
    35→[Er99] Erdős, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_.
    36→Matematiche (Catania) (1999).
    37→-/
    38→
    39→open SimpleGraph
    40→
    41→namespace Erdos611
    42→
    43→/-- $S$ is a maximal clique of $G$ (as a `Finset`): it is a clique and no vertex
    44→    outside $S$ can be added while preserving the clique property. -/
    45→def IsMaximalCliqueFS {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    46→  G.IsClique (S : Set (Fin n)) ∧
    47→  ∀ v : Fin n, v ∉ S → ¬G.IsClique (↑(insert v S) : Set (Fin n))
    48→
    49→/-- $T$ is a clique transversal of $G$ if $T$ meets every maximal clique of $G$
    50→    that has at least $2$ vertices. -/
    51→def IsCliqueTransversal {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (T : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    52→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), IsMaximalCliqueFS G S → 2 ≤ S.card → (T ∩ S).Nonempty
    53→
    54→/-- The clique transversal number $\tau(G)$: the minimum cardinality of a clique
    55→    transversal of $G$. -/
    56→noncomputable def cliqueTransversalNum {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
    57→  sInf { k : ℕ | ∃ T : Finset (Fin n), IsCliqueTransversal G T ∧ T.card = k }
    58→
    59→/-- Every maximal clique of $G$ with at least $2$ vertices has at least $m$ vertices. -/
    60→def AllMaxCliquesAtLeast {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (m : ℕ) : Prop :=
    61→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), IsMaximalCliqueFS G S → 2 ≤ S.card → m ≤ S.card
    62→
    63→/--
    64→**Erdős Problem 611** (Main conjecture) [EGT92][Er94][Er99]:
    65→
    66→If all maximal cliques in $G$ have at least $cn$ vertices then $\tau(G) = o_c(n)$.
    67→
    68→Formulated as: for every $c > 0$ and $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all
    69→$n \ge N_0$, every graph $G$ on $n$ vertices in which every maximal clique has at
    70→least $\lceil c \cdot n \rceil$ vertices satisfies $\tau(G) \le \varepsilon \cdot n$.
    71→-/
    72→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    73→theorem erdos_611 (c : ℝ) (hc : 0 < c) (hc1 : c ≤ 1) :
    74→    ∀ ε : ℝ, ε > 0 →
    75→    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, n ≥ N₀ →
    76→      ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    77→        AllMaxCliquesAtLeast G (⌈c * (n : ℝ)⌉₊) →
    78→        (cliqueTransversalNum G : ℝ) ≤ ε * (n : ℝ) := by
    79→  sorry
    80→
    81→/--
    82→**Erdős Problem 611** (Known bound, Erdős-Gallai-Tuza) [EGT92]:
    83→
    84→If every maximal clique of $G$ on $n$ vertices has at least $k$ vertices then
    85→$\tau(G) \le n - \sqrt{kn}$.
    86→-/
    87→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    88→theorem erdos_611.variants.known_bound (n : ℕ) (hn : n ≥ 1)
    89→    (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (k : ℕ) (hk : k ≥ 1)
    90→    (hclique : AllMaxCliquesAtLeast G k) :

    97→If every maximal clique of $G$ on $n$ vertices has at least $n + 3 - 2\sqrt{n}$ vertices
    98→then $\tau(G) \le 1$. (This threshold is best possible.)
    99→-/

    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 151
    21→
    22→Erdős and Gallai conjectured that the clique transversal number of any graph on $n$ vertices
    23→is at most $n - H(n)$, where $H(n)$ is the minimum independence number over all triangle-free
    24→graphs on $n$ vertices.
    25→
    26→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/151](https://www.erdosproblems.com/151)
    27→
    28→[Er88] Erdős, P., _Problems and results on chromatic numbers in finite and infinite graphs_,
    29→1988, p.82.
    30→
    31→[EGT92] Erdős, P., Gallai, T. and Tuza, Zs., _Covering the cliques of a graph with vertices_,
    32→1992, p.280.
    33→-/
    34→
    35→open SimpleGraph
    36→
    37→namespace Erdos151
    38→
    39→/-- $S$ is a maximal clique of $G$ (represented as a `Finset`): it is a clique and
    40→no vertex outside $S$ can be added while preserving the clique property. -/
    41→def IsMaximalCliqueFS {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    42→  G.IsClique (S : Set (Fin n)) ∧
    43→  ∀ v : Fin n, v ∉ S → ¬G.IsClique (↑(insert v S) : Set (Fin n))
    44→
    45→/-- $T$ is a clique transversal of $G$ if $T$ has non-empty intersection with every
    46→maximal clique of $G$ that has at least $2$ vertices. -/
    47→def IsCliqueTransversal {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (T : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    48→  ∀ S : Finset (Fin n), IsMaximalCliqueFS G S → 2 ≤ S.card → (T ∩ S).Nonempty
    49→
    50→/-- The clique transversal number $\tau(G)$: the minimum cardinality of a clique
    51→transversal of $G$. -/
    52→noncomputable def cliqueTransversalNumber {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
    53→  sInf { k : ℕ | ∃ T : Finset (Fin n), IsCliqueTransversal G T ∧ T.card = k }
    54→
    55→/-- $S$ is an independent set in $G$: no two distinct vertices of $S$ are adjacent. -/
    56→def IsIndependentSet {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    57→  ∀ u v : Fin n, u ∈ S → v ∈ S → u ≠ v → ¬G.Adj u v
    58→
    59→/-- The independence number $\alpha(G)$: the maximum cardinality of an independent set. -/
    60→noncomputable def independenceNumber {n : ℕ} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ℕ :=
    61→  sSup { k : ℕ | ∃ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ∧ S.card = k }
    62→
    63→/-- $H(n)$ is maximal such that every triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices contains
    64→an independent set of size $H(n)$; equivalently, $H(n)$ is the minimum
    65→independence number over all triangle-free graphs on $n$ vertices. -/
    66→noncomputable def H (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    67→  sInf { k : ℕ | ∃ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree 3 ∧ independenceNumber G = k }
    68→
    69→/--
    70→Erdős Problem 151 [Er88, p.82] [EGT92, p.280] (problem of Erdős and Gallai):
    71→If $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices then $\tau(G) \leq n - H(n)$,
    72→where $\tau(G)$ is the clique transversal number of $G$ and $H(n)$ is the maximum $k$
    73→such that every triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices contains an independent set
    74→of size $k$.
    75→-/
    76→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    77→theorem erdos_151 : answer(sorry) ↔
    78→    ∀ (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    79→      cliqueTransversalNumber G ≤ n - H n := by
    80→  sorry
    81→
    82→end Erdos151
    83→

   331→/-! ### Graphs without cliques -/
   332→
   333→
   334→section CliqueFree
   335→
   336→variable {m n : ℕ}
   337→
   338→/-- `G.CliqueFree n` means that `G` has no `n`-cliques. -/
   339→def CliqueFree (n : ℕ) : Prop :=
   340→  ∀ t, ¬G.IsNClique n t
   341→
   342→variable {G H} {s : Finset α}
   343→
   344→theorem IsNClique.not_cliqueFree (hG : G.IsNClique n s) : ¬G.CliqueFree n :=
   345→  fun h ↦ h _ hG
   346→
   347→theorem not_cliqueFree_of_top_embedding {n : ℕ} (f : (⊤ : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) ↪g G) :
   348→    ¬G.CliqueFree n := by
   349→  simp only [CliqueFree, isNClique_iff, isClique_iff_induce_eq, not_forall, Classical.not_not]
   350→  use Finset.univ.map f.toEmbedding
   351→  simp only [card_map, Finset.card_fin, and_true]
   352→  ext ⟨v, hv⟩ ⟨w, hw⟩
   353→  simp only [coe_map, Set.mem_image, coe_univ, Set.mem_univ, true_and] at hv hw
   354→  obtain ⟨v', rfl⟩ := hv
   355→  obtain ⟨w', rfl⟩ := hw
   356→  simp_rw [RelEmbedding.coe_toEmbedding, comap_adj, Function.Embedding.coe_subtype, f.map_adj_iff,
   357→    top_adj, ne_eq, Subtype.mk.injEq, RelEmbedding.inj]
   358→
   359→/-- An embedding of a complete graph that witnesses the fact that the graph is not clique-free. -/
   360→noncomputable def topEmbeddingOfNotCliqueFree {n : ℕ} (h : ¬G.CliqueFree n) :
   361→    (⊤ : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) ↪g G := by
   362→  simp only [CliqueFree, isNClique_iff, isClique_iff_induce_eq, not_forall, Classical.not_not] at h
   363→  obtain ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h.choose_spec
   364→  have : (⊤ : SimpleGraph (Fin #h.choose)) ≃g (⊤ : SimpleGraph h.choose) := by
   365→    apply Iso.completeGraph
   366→    simpa using (Fintype.equivFin h.choose).symm
   367→  rw [← ha] at this
   368→  convert (Embedding.induce ↑h.choose).comp this.toEmbedding
   369→  exact hb.symm
   370→
   371→theorem not_cliqueFree_iff (n : ℕ) : ¬G.CliqueFree n ↔ Nonempty (completeGraph (Fin n) ↪g G) :=
   372→  ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨topEmbeddingOfNotCliqueFree h⟩, fun ⟨f⟩ ↦ not_cliqueFree_of_top_embedding f⟩
   373→
   374→theorem cliqueFree_iff {n : ℕ} : G.CliqueFree n ↔ IsEmpty (completeGraph (Fin n) ↪g G) := by
   375→  contrapose!; exact not_cliqueFree_iff n
   376→
   377→/-- A simple graph has no `card β`-cliques iff it does not contain `⊤ : SimpleGraph β`. -/
   378→theorem cliqueFree_iff_top_free {β : Type*} [Fintype β] :
   379→    G.CliqueFree (card β) ↔ (⊤ : SimpleGraph β).Free G := by
   380→  rw [← not_iff_not, not_free, not_cliqueFree_iff,
   381→    isContained_congr (Iso.completeGraph (Fintype.equivFin β)) Iso.refl]
   382→  exact ⟨fun ⟨f⟩ ↦ ⟨f.toCopy⟩, fun ⟨f⟩ ↦ ⟨f.topEmbedding⟩⟩
   383→
   384→theorem not_cliqueFree_card_of_top_embedding [Fintype α] (f : (⊤ : SimpleGraph α) ↪g G) :
   385→    ¬G.CliqueFree (card α) := by
   386→  rw [not_cliqueFree_iff]
   387→  exact ⟨(Iso.completeGraph (Fintype.equivFin α)).symm.toEmbedding.trans f⟩
   388→
   389→@[simp] lemma not_cliqueFree_zero : ¬ G.CliqueFree 0 :=
   390→  fun h ↦ h ∅ <| isNClique_empty.mpr rfl
   391→
   392→@[simp]
   393→theorem cliqueFree_bot (h : 2 ≤ n) : (⊥ : SimpleGraph α).CliqueFree n := by
   394→  intro t ht
   395→  have := le_trans h (isNClique_bot_iff.1 ht).1
   396→  contradiction
   397→
   398→theorem CliqueFree.mono (h : m ≤ n) : G.CliqueFree m → G.CliqueFree n := by
   399→  intro hG s hs
   400→  obtain ⟨t, hts, ht⟩ := exists_subset_card_eq (h.trans hs.card_eq.ge)
   401→  exact hG _ ⟨hs.isClique.subset hts, ht⟩
   402→
   403→theorem CliqueFree.anti (h : G ≤ H) : H.CliqueFree n → G.CliqueFree n :=
   404→  forall_imp fun _ ↦ mt <| IsNClique.mono h
   405→
   406→/-- If a graph is cliquefree, any graph that embeds into it is also cliquefree. -/
   407→theorem CliqueFree.comap {H : SimpleGraph β} (f : H ↪g G) : G.CliqueFree n → H.CliqueFree n := by
   408→  intro h; contrapose h
   409→  exact not_cliqueFree_of_top_embedding <| f.comp (topEmbeddingOfNotCliqueFree h)
   410→
   411→@[simp] theorem cliqueFree_map_iff {f : α ↪ β} [Nonempty α] :
   412→    (G.map f).CliqueFree n ↔ G.CliqueFree n := by
   413→  obtain (hle | hlt) := le_or_gt n 1
   414→  · obtain (rfl | rfl) := Nat.le_one_iff_eq_zero_or_eq_one.1 hle
   415→    · simp [CliqueFree]
   416→    simp [CliqueFree, show ∃ (_ : β), True from ⟨f (Classical.arbitrary _), trivial⟩]
   417→  simp [CliqueFree, isNClique_map_iff hlt]
   418→
   419→/-- See `SimpleGraph.cliqueFree_of_chromaticNumber_lt` for a tighter bound. -/
   420→theorem cliqueFree_of_card_lt [Fintype α] (hc : card α < n) : G.CliqueFree n := by
   421→  rw [cliqueFree_iff]
   422→  contrapose! hc
   423→  simpa only [Fintype.card_fin] using Fintype.card_le_of_embedding hc.some.toEmbedding
   424→
   425→/-- A complete `r`-partite graph has no `n`-cliques for `r < n`. -/
   426→theorem cliqueFree_completeMultipartiteGraph {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] (V : ι → Type*)
   427→    (hc : card ι < n) : (completeMultipartiteGraph V).CliqueFree n := by
   428→  rw [cliqueFree_iff, isEmpty_iff]
   429→  intro f
   430→  obtain ⟨v, w, hn, he⟩ := exists_ne_map_eq_of_card_lt (Sigma.fst ∘ f) (by simp [hc])
   431→  rw [← top_adj, ← f.map_adj_iff, comap_adj, top_adj] at hn
   432→  exact absurd he hn
   433→
   434→namespace completeMultipartiteGraph
   435→
   436→variable {ι : Type*} (V : ι → Type*)
   437→
   438→/-- Embedding of the complete graph on `ι` into `completeMultipartiteGraph` on `ι` nonempty parts -/
   439→@[simps]
   440→def topEmbedding (f : ∀ (i : ι), V i) :
   441→    (⊤ : SimpleGraph ι) ↪g completeMultipartiteGraph V where
   442→  toFun := fun i ↦ ⟨i, f i⟩
   443→  inj' := fun _ _ h ↦ (Sigma.mk.inj_iff.1 h).1
   444→  map_rel_iff' := by simp
   445→
   446→theorem not_cliqueFree_of_le_card [Fintype ι] (f : ∀ (i : ι), V i) (hc : n ≤ Fintype.card ι) :
   447→    ¬ (completeMultipartiteGraph V).CliqueFree n :=
   448→  fun hf ↦ (cliqueFree_iff.1 <| hf.mono hc).elim' <|
